mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS signals stronger household spending, lifting near-term corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of out-of-sample months or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5% while CPIAUCSL inflation is still accelerating, discount-rate pressure dominates earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds its 12-month inflation rate
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage point over three months signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage point over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a positive 60-day S&P 500 return frequency above 55% or if the triggered mean 60-day return is not lower than the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 6-month momentum in INDPRO indicates improving production and operating leverage, raising expected margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not associated with higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO momentum in walk-forward tests, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in HOUST while FEDFUNDS is restrictive transmits higher financing costs into housing demand and bank-credit risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% year over year while FEDFUNDS is above 3.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not underperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return by at least 50 bps out of sample or if positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 combined with non-accelerating CPIAUCSL identifies real growth without inflation pressure, improving expected real cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is below its 12-month inflation rate
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations fail to deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of out-of-sample cases or if the triggered average return is not above the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A downside crossing in CPIAUCSL inflation with FEDFUNDS not rising signals improving policy optionality and lower inflation risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses below 2.5% while FEDFUNDS has not increased over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of cases or if the average triggered return is not higher than baseline by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Very strong PAYEMS growth when FEDFUNDS is already above 3% increases fears of renewed wage inflation and tighter policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly gain crosses above 250000 persons while FEDFUNDS is above 3%
regime_hint: hot labor market under restrictive policy
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if event-window returns are not below unconditional 5-day returns in walk-forward evaluation.
